reviews.review_text: 100% the best co-op RPG experience out there. No contest. No competitors

it's a tough recommend from me.

honestly, i played divinity original sin 2 before this game and if you had a lot of fun with the second title, just don't bother playing this game. it certainly has it's charms (and a very high difficulty spike) but it just isn't really the same, if you're looking for the same experience as before.

i however really found myself enjoying it, especially in the third act of the game, and i got to like the story more than expected. it's weird and awkward, sometimes even boring, but it has a unique flair about it. also, i would recommend playing it with one of your favorite friends, so you can laugh and cry together at least.

but yeah, have fun with divinity: original sin. just don't expect it to be like the second part, and especially not like baldur's gate lol.

I really wish I could recommend this game, but I just can't. I currently only play to reach Geraldine!

1. Playing on normal difficulty causes death during pretty much every battle.
2. There are, in my opinion, very little combo attack options for your party (unlike BG3)
3. I like doing side quests, but when they become almost mandatory just to level up to be able to survive battles, something is wrong.
4. The crafting system is insane. I expected more books to learn recipes and was thoroughly disappointed to find out one has to test different combos to see what happens. With the amount of items in the game, this just turned into a horrible chore - which I refuse to dabble in (hello internet).
5. The UI is "physically" transparent. If something is behind the menu (items on tables, floor, ground etc) the cursor will highlight the items instead of the menu items.
6. Music cuts off mid battle every once in a while.
7. Cursor glitches every so often in battle, making you move to a new spot instead of attacking.
8. Autosaves triggered the first couple of hours, but rarely trigger anymore (155 or so hours, split on 2 playthroughs). 

The game has dry, naughty humour though, which I like.

This game has some of the most fun combat ever and has genuinely given me hilarious memories on par with some of my best outings with my friends. I look back on this game very fondly! The main story is quite a slog, however.

Annoyingly this game is designed around co-op and the single player suffers for it.

At the start of the game you create two characters and they're both apart of your group.
You'll encounter events where the two characters you created will converse about the things they've observed. Then you will have dialogue options for your two characters. 

I foolishly answered the prompts as if I were asked them. For each character. So every time it came up my two characters were always in unanimous agreement.
Found out half way through that the choices you make reward you with stats. So by roleplaying the character I wanted to, I was punished by missing out on essential stats.


This game is also extremely linear. 
Missions only have 1 solution.
Even though there's an "open world" there's a very clear level progression that you're supposed to follow. If you skip a zone you'll be behind on xp for the rest of the game since there's a finite amount of enemies and the game is balanced around you killing every enemy on the map.

The "tactian" mode description should be saying:

"Nobody loves you, you are alone, everybody is going to spit on you, you won't achieve anything in your miserable life, everyone and everything is better than you, you'll die alone in suffering! This is not hard mode - it's cheat mode!"

I've picked tactician because at some point I've started playing games on hard mode for fun. Remembering playing DOS2 years ago as lone wolf - solo I've decided some challenge on hard mode in DOS1.

But this is just ridiculous how every thing that worked as counter measure in DOS1 classic was nerfed / blocked in EE.

I'm just tired of running with 8 lvl 20 chests to block npcs pathing for cheesing this broken mess.

Other than that the game is pretty much fun (aside from hours of suffering) but not on broken tactic mode.

This game is a bible of information. There is so much lore that I space out when I play. The voice acting is really funny but the game play is a bit out of date and at times extremely frustrating. I've been having a lot of fun with this game. 8/10
Very challenging but fun and addicting. A very well done tactical RPG. Definitely a must play if you like Baldur's Gate 3 and have never played this and it's sequel.

if i see one more trap that one shots....

On a serious note, the game's combat is its biggest charm, one I could call the 'secret sauce.' If that makes sense. But the story isn't particularly good; though some parts (Jahan especially) are pretty well-written, and if you're up for Python shenanigans, why not

[h1] An Amazing RPG w/ Too Many Traps & Puzzles[/h1]

[b] Pros: [/b]

(1) The writing in this game is top tier, fully voice-acted. There are weird tonal shifts in this game where you go from super serious story plots to extremely silly npc interactions, but it's all just really well voice-acted regardless. The quality here really elevates things and made some characters super memorable. 
(2) I really enjoyed the character building process in this game, having the freedom to invest wherever you please and craft your own unique build regardless of "class" decision at the beginning was nice. The game also allows you enough levels in that you have the flexibility to make dips into other skills instead of feeling siloed. 
(3) Visuals and performance wise, this game holds up incredible, I have a good rig but I can assure you this game will run on any half-competent modern system. 
(4) I'm sure many would consider this a con, but I actually quite enjoyed the "basic journal that gives you no hints for quests" in this game. It's classic Larian, you gotta read and investigate everything! The game will not hold your hands to find answers which I think is awesome. 

[b] Cons: [/b] 

(1) [b] Whoever tf at Larian decided to put mines and sentinels (ESPECIALLY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ MINES) increasingly throughout the game, I hate you, that is all. [/b]
(2) While combat in this game is good, don't get me wrong, there are certain mechanics I experienced as a mage that literally just piss me off: 
     -  While largely improved in the second game, visibility here is just bad. The smallest little cloud of smoke can prevent you from casting a spell on somebody RIGHT IN FRONT OF YOU. This will happen to you A LOT throughout the game. 
     - Not sure if this would be a geometry problem or not, but I constantly had issues placing certain spells like Ice Wall because it wouldn't deem certain areas as "placable" for the wall even though I or an enemy can walk on that space just fine. Keep in mind the SMOKE MENTIONED ABOVE also affects this. 
     - The mechanics for the charm spell feel broken as a charmed enemy will literally just screw you over more often than not in the ladder half of the game by healing summons, itself, or healing other enemies by just hitting them with an element they are immune too... Keep in mind charm, like many conditions, only last 2 turns..
(3) The amount of puzzles this game has is just unnecessary. Not only that, in the final act of the game, it's basically all puzzles! I'm not even kidding here, the last 10-20% of this game is basically 90% puzzle 10% actual fighting timewise. It's ridiculous and honestly made the otherwise really good ending feel like a slog to get to. 
(4) I played through this game with a friend with them being the host. At a certain point I stopped caring but there would be constant small levels of desync like health bars not displaying correctly, stats not being the correct value, etc. 

[b] Conclusion [/b]

Overall I sound super negative (Cause this game did absolutely make my blood boil at times) but I mean it when I say Larian killed it in the writing and voice acting department, which again with the fun combat makes this still an absolute recommend for fans of CRPGs.

I just can't really recommend this game, I'm sure there might be a fun game in there somewhere... but I didn't find it on my 20 or so hours on both steam and PS4. Maybe I will come back to it someday but if after that many hours I'm still not having any fun, then I think I'm going to move on.

Overall, I really enjoyed this game.  I'm a huge CRPG fan and this hit right in that sweet spot for me.  However, at a certain late game point, the quality drops significantly (in my view).  It goes from a really good, fun exploration/tactical combat experience to a slow, drawn out slog.  If you liked D:OS2 (and played them out of order like me) or other CRPGs you'll like this game, just be aware that the ending isn't the strongest part of this title.

Great game! If you love DnD & BG3 this game is for you! Be prepared to find so much equipment as you play... lots of organizing. I recommend not buying anything until lvl 10. 
Only complaint is once you get close to the end it felt like they just kept extending the story.

if youre up for a long adventure where you have to figure out where to go and what to do yourself then this game might suit you. This game was a lot more fun then i thought but only buy it if its on sale tho.
I hope this game does not put its best foot forward, because this opening is not good. After two hours, I really have no desire to continue with this adventure. The sequel seems way better.
After Pathfinder Kingmaker continuing my CRPG journey. Also good experience in steam deck.

insanely janky by todays standards but deal with it and play it

just use a guide on occasion an youll be fine

The game mechanics look enticing but lack execution.  There's nothing more frustrating than investing time in character creation, strategizing growth, etc only to turn the entire first town hostile for attempting to help them fight a fire with a water barrel you somehow place incorrectly.

Ill admit it took me quite a while to actually get into it but once i did i got to say its really a very good RPG with great lore and fun to play just has a bit of a learning curve when im finished with this one im 100% buying the second one

My rating is positive because the game definitely stands out from "the crowd". But a lot of things didn't work here.
First of all, the pace of combat can be tedious. There are many bugs, some quite basic, like the wrong order of initiative portraits, or textures that break through or are missing. Skills and traits that do not work etc.
The game is unbalanced in terms of difficulty. Final fight are nothing compared to first act.
Stupid design decisions like having to persuade teammates to make a decision. Useless reputation and character systems. 12 blood stones to finish game. And most importantly, especially the final battles. They're epic and the ending is good, but the game completely favors playing with fire and earth over water and air. It's a disaster.
The game is so fun for me and my brother to play together with! It really fulfill our expectations about role-playing games! Can't wait to spend more time playing it!
Difficult beginning and can sometimes be a bit of a drag, but still well worth it.

I believe this is the first CRPG i complete, I heard really good thing about Larian and chose this game as my starting point, I'll play the sequel in a few weeks or months. 
For starters i really like the graphics, the art style seems from a old story, the story is nice but i started to rush trough it by half of Hunter's edge map. I like the companions they are Fun, i ended up choosing the most use full ones, Madora and Jahan. I love the liberty this game gives you on approaching problem solving quest completion and combat.
The puzzles are entertaining and very enjoyable, i was to dumb for the last one.
Some fights are really unbalanced, like Braccus Rex insta kill in the first turn, I ended up cheesing him by jumping him at the door of his arena. 
Most of my problems stem from Hunters edge, I don't know if this happens for everyone but my game started to break a little, some dialogues didn't trigger sound, one or two quests broke down and wouldn't update despite meeting the requirements, the balance of fights was also missing, all of the Hunters Edge map  was like a walk in the park, I killed everything in 2 or 3 turns, the only thing that ended up killing me were some mud crabs of all things, while the boss of Jahan's quest the super demon ended up frozen on the floor trough all of his boss fight jajaja (killed him in 3 or 2 turns). Some enemies would just do nothing on their turn just stand there wasting time.
The final bosses I really like the first 2, the final one is really frustrating, the issue is not that it does a tone of damage the issue is the NPC that you have to keep alive when it dies in 2 turns and all the summons of the boss attack her 3 times per turn and she doesn't move from there. I ended up cheesing him by reloading the same turn until soulslap triggered and even then I only won because the boss decided it was more important to kill the spider i summoned on accident, love you spider thanks for tanking all the attacks in those 2 turns couldn't have done it without you.
At the end of the day I had a lot of fun with this game, rally got into the experience of this genre.
OG Larian Studios-esque game. If you like Baldur's Gate 3, give the Divinity Original Sin games a try, starting with this one of course.

Thus has ended my first experience of this game and it really does not dissappoints me. 

Yes there is lots to be refined despite it being enhanced edition from the bugs (oh dear lord it's annoying) to some of the janky mechanics (that i'd say because of its age) but nonetheless it is really fun to experience that im thankful that this is the first one i ever played.

Would really recommend this game to everybody, make them really experience the greatness  that it is.

Solid game, Some of the late game puzzles are just absolute ♥♥♥♥, alot of areas seem like a literal minefield with traps every 20 steps, And the damn sentinels. But the quality of the writing, voice acting, and combat make up for all the flaws. except maybe the source temple, that place is ass.

For a game as overshadowed by its sequel as this one, I'm glad to say that Original Sin 1 was an incredibly fun time. Good story with memorable characters, sidequests that go from hilarious to heartbreaking, cool worldbuilding and lore to learn, combat and progression systems that are fun to mess around with, even if a bit too simple at times

Some puzzles and navigation, especially around traps and environmental hazards, can be frustrating at times, but it doesn't distract much from all the other positives this game has going on

I had no reason to doubt this game...and this game has it own features that can help it hold its ground, I mean Baldur's Gate 3 can still beat this game due to the cinematics, graphics and all but considering the age of this game, it's still a CB, aka Certified Banger. I'll give to a 7.2 sea shells outta 10
Its old but simple which makes things easy to follow. but wow the crashes are terrible and frequent and only allowing 1 mod at a time for a game this old is terrible. because it needs the qol mods.

This game isn't perfect by any means, but it offers a ton of character customization and offers plenty of challenging and enjoyable CRPG combat. If you take the time to do as many quests as you can, just one playthrough is quite lengthy, and there's plenty of room for additional playthroughs on harder difficulties, with different builds, etc. Overall, well-worth playing, and good enough that I'll be playing the sequel.

Its a mixed bag! The story is pretty generic good vs evil yada yada, I liked the bits that stepped out of typical fantasy fair with steampunk and robotic elements but they were few and far between. Thankfully the game has a pretty lighthearted and comedic tone to a lot of its scenes that helps keep it entertaining, rarely laugh out loud funny but it was charming (personal favorite is the MLM adventurer's guild you can deal with), but it's not enough to completely stop the feeling of the game reaaaaally dragging on by the final act

Combat absolutely redeems it though, I had a lot of fun with the environment interactions and status effects, freezing water to make enemies slip or opening combat by shooting explosive arrows at oil barrels, deciding spells and arrow types based on weather, it really makes you consider combat arenas and look at your tools available especially on higher difficulties. 

Great tactical combat game, not so good roleplaying game, but you can see the bones they built on for a much better sequel! Wish it was a bit shorter and that it did more the initial premise of fantasy murder mystery before it became "save the world epic quest etc etc"

Very solid CRPG. I played it back around its launch, so I imagine if you're coming from BG3 or DOS:2, it might feel a bit dated, but it's still worth a play. Only bad thing I'll say is that it took me a few hours to really get into it, but after that I was fully immersed. I remember the first quest being a bit confusing, but that might've just been because I was new to the genre at that point.

overrated. generally unclear objectives. feels like I'm regularly doing ~5 minutes of combat and then an hour of walking/puzzles/dialogue. Speaking of the dialogue... way too long and boring. Really slows down the pace. I often read it faster than it's narrated... some of these voice actors talk SUUUUPEEEERRRRR SLLLLOOOOOWWWWW. I honestly don't know how it's rated 90%+ positive. It's more of a 75% kind of game to me, tops. I ended up not finishing because there were 4 separate main questlines (I think?) that all became too frustrating to continue. Not because it was too challenging, or difficult, or even cryptic, because that would imply it's clever. But more so because it's crappily designed so that you're constantly ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ around with random things until you figure out how to proceed. Most of it is guesswork on what you're supposed to do. Actually had to reload from an earlier save because a critical item didn't drop after a boss fight (~5 hours wasted; somehow I initiated the fight the first time without any of the dialogue). It wasn't pointed out to me either by the game; I just ended up having a hunch that's what happened after exhausting all other viable options. And I don't expect a game to hold my hand but maybe mark things, or offer something beyond "it seems unsafe, I better watch my step." Wow, thanks. I suppose it could be argued that I could always use a guide... but I personally don't use guides. If a game is so poorly designed that I need to refer to a guide to get through it without getting frustrated, then it's too poorly designed to be decently playable. Kind of takes the sport of out of it. 

Probably too many different gear options too. And picking it up doesn't seem to be very intuitive... nothing is marked until you hover over it, and sometimes a giant enemy is laying across your drops, obscuring it. Actually so much of it is obscured in general by terrain too. 

There seems to be a decent game somewhere in all this convoluted mess. Should've made dialogue less frilly, should've made objectives clearly stated, should've used basic common sense when it comes to how drops are handled, and should probably have under the hood game-checks that makes sure you don't glitch yourself out of being able to advance the questlines. Just plays like they went for "deep and rich" and ended up with "overly-complicated and painful", but were so convinced that they had the former that they didn't bother to address the latter. Was so bad that I made sure to add the other copy of this game to my hidden list so I don't accidentally play it again when I'm too old and senile to remember how bad this game was.

Once you've played DOS1/2 and BG3 you realize how much garbage Larian recycles for every game. Special elemental arrows, ground hazards and elemental interactions are always the same. Pathetically lazy dev.

I have a theory that games are generally made up of: (1) gameplay, (2) mechanics, (3) writing (includes dialogue and narrative), and (4) charm. I also have a theory that sometimes really good stories are unfortunately trapped in bad games, and vice-versa. This is a prime example of a GREAT story trapped in a sub-par game. 

This is a classic turn-based CRPG, the gameplay is bog standard. But there are janky issues: the cursor sways in combat but does not in the overworld. So I would sometimes miss and attack the floor (wizard) or a pillar/boulder (set piece dressing) because I timed it wrong. I played this multiplayer with a friend (he hosted), and I had a recurring bug where the party was at full health but the health bars would only reflect 3/4 health, so I was healing like an idiot. The pathing was not fantastic, so sometimes the player characters would walk through fire or poison and damage (or kill) themselves. It wasn't often, but it happened enough that it was certainly frustrating (especially when I had the resurrect spell late game but we had to use resurrect scrolls instead). 

A lot of mechanics were also unpredictable in almost predictable ways. I would use the expert marksman skill ricochet, which would bounce off of one enemy onto others. Except, sometimes, it would avoid enemies half a meter next to my target, bounce 10 meters (very long distance) to an enemy behind set dressing, hit the set dressing (in this example, a string and comically large wooden pin in a tent), and would be a waste of a skill. I would use my air wand, and at some point it would jut electricity and inevitably stun one of my friend's characters for a turn or two. I couldn't know exactly when, but I knew it would happen. 

Little, janky things that got in the way of an extremely charming game, with extremely charming characters (loved the rats and the chicken, for example). The story is super interesting. So, great story, very charming, but not a great /game/, per se. 

I would rate it somewhere around a 6.5/10. Definitely worth playing for the story. If you like games like Fallout, Rule of Rose, Silent Hill 2, or like, Nier, DEFINITELY worth it. Especially with a friend.

Stayed for the great mechanics and gameplay. Whilst the overarching story is a bit bland, its all of the side quests and characters that make it worthwhile to explore and complete as much as possible.

Tedious, frustrating & obtuse 

are my takeaways after my 2nd and final attempt. Loved the original Divinity series, but this one feels like a fight with the interface and rather a bit of a slog, especially in battle. Normally I don't mind looking & walking around, discovering, or doing some battle positioning and such; but here, when you are also constantly fighting the egregious camera (designed that way and now unmoddable in EE) and fumbling interface...

It's too bad, it otherwise seems to tick all the boxes I look for. Now, it's just wasted time and money.

Nice RPG with interactive environment

Becomes a bit samey after you get fireball

🎮 Finished? ✅ 

Loved the story, but the mechanics were rough as hell. I definitely struggled. The final fight was so difficult that I nearly quit.

That said, I did enjoy the plot, and the characters were intriguing. I liked the art and audio design a lot.
Didn't understand the game and Turn based combat feels so artificial. Only buy if you enjoy Turn Based Combat otherwise just forget that such a game even exists.

Look... idk, I really wanted to enjoy this. My friend and I put over 200 hours in BG3 - but this just isn't as good. I'm glad Larian worked out the wrinkles, but I can't imagine trying to play this again.

Upon entering the main square, you will be approached by an individual who, for who knows what reason in the world, wants your consent to steal a fish from a stall.

You have only two options: Encourage it or not. Like a good thief, he'll go with whatever you decide. He's a good boy.

If you decide to convince him not to steal the fish, the fish remains in the cart, which you can pick up without it counting as stealing.  The fish seller will see what happened and thank you for robbing her.
-----
When you knock down an enemy, their model clips into the ground. 50% of the time you are waiting for a poison cloud, pool of fire, or pool of poison to disappear because everything is a explosive barrel booby trap. Don't bother to get lockpicking or trap disarming, because you can't spare any levels for anything other than combat, and if you build wrong, better make a new save file. You will have 50 different crafting components and roughly 0 recipes to spend it on, because you NEED to be introduced to hollow grenade casings and headless arrows on the spawn beach.

I got this game for -3 dollar in a bundle and I wish I paid more without the bundle instead so I wouldn't want to finish this.
This game ain't it. The outfit design is straight-up gross, and the story has way too many dudes — it's like a nasty smell everywhere. Stick around too long, and the later maps get hella boring too.
Excellent stuff from Larian studios as always
die hard fan girl. i love this game and just about everything else Larian has done.

RED AND ORANGE, YELLER AND GREEN, THE FINEST VEGGIES CYSEAL’S EVER SEEN!

JOIN THE SPUD CLUB IF YOU’VE GOT AN EYE FOR FINE POTATERS!

LETTUCE SEE IF YOU CAN CHOOSE THE FINEST GREENS!

NO BETTER SOLUTION FER THE ZOMBIE PROBLEM THAN A SET OF SILVER ARRAHS!

POTIONS TO BEMUSCLE YOU, SCROLLS TO ENTUSSLE YOU!
AMAZING engine right from the start. No wonder why Larian is where they are now
Would play again, had I the time, though the UI is a bit lacking.
Bro my guy died in first 10min of game help me revive him, game hasn't even started yet T-T
uh i like that bg3 game and this like that but good in own way
amazing game played bg3 then the divinity series hyped for dos 3
Great game for fans of thinking RPGs
Ending boss fight was MAD annoying
Um dos melhores RPGs que eu já joguei.
Wow, that was A JOURNEY! I am speechless in a good way :)
great game if you hate yourself
Great! Love this goofy-ass adventure!
It's slow, I have to come back to it
Great game and story.
Very good playing coop
Cool predecessor to D:OS2, enjoyed it.
its like not fun
Bless the Seven, what a journey!
30eur is good price for this masterpiece
